purpose: Planning artifact for Valgaron place category relationships, category-specific fields, and linkable cross-entry fields.

scope.sourceCategorySet: Canonical category nodes in this artifact's tree section
notes: Fields marked link should become relationship-backed selectors rather than plain text when the editor supports typed fields.
Cardinality is editorial guidance, not a database constraint for the current local prototype.

relationshipTypes.label: contains
relationshipTypes.description: Explicit outbound collection of child places for editor convenience.

relationshipTypes.label: capital of
relationshipTypes.description: A city, town, castle, fortress, or seat serving as a capital for a polity.

relationshipTypes.label: controlled by
relationshipTypes.description: Current political, military, religious, or personal control.

relationshipTypes.label: claimed by
relationshipTypes.description: Disputed or symbolic ownership, sovereignty, or inheritance.

relationshipTypes.label: bordered by
relationshipTypes.description: Adjacency, shared border, coastline, or physical edge.

relationshipTypes.label: connected to
relationshipTypes.description: Travel, trade, river, road, pass, gate, or transit connection.

relationshipTypes.label: route between
relationshipTypes.description: A road, pass, river, sea lane, or space lane that links endpoints.

relationshipTypes.label: flows through
relationshipTypes.description: Hydrological path through regions, settlements, or terrain.

relationshipTypes.label: flows into
relationshipTypes.description: River, channel, tide, lava, glacier, or magical current destination.

relationshipTypes.label: part of
relationshipTypes.description: Component relationship when strict containment is too strong.

relationshipTypes.label: site of
relationshipTypes.description: Events, legends, rituals, discoveries, disasters, or origin myths tied to a place.

relationshipTypes.label: home of
relationshipTypes.description: Residence, homeland, headquarters, nesting ground, or cultural base.

relationshipTypes.label: founded by
relationshipTypes.description: Founder, founding culture, parent colony, or founding event.

relationshipTypes.label: built by
relationshipTypes.description: Builder, engineering faction, original culture, patron, or construction event.

relationshipTypes.label: created by
relationshipTypes.description: Creator, maker, originating event, spell, technology, or mythic cause.

relationshipTypes.label: references
relationshipTypes.description: Place-specific magic, customs, hazards, artifacts, faith, law, ecology, or technology.

alternateNames.label: Alternate names
alternateNames.description: Exonyms, historical names, nicknames, translations, or faction-specific names.

category.label: Place category
category.description: One of the supported place categories.

region.label: Region
region.description: Loose regional label for early drafting before a place has more specific parent links.

significance.label: Significance
significance.description: Why the place matters in the world, plot, culture, or geography.

status.label: Draft status
status.description: Draft, canon, needs review, deprecated, or archived.

settlements.label: Settlements
settlements.description: Named inhabited places within this place.

neighbors.label: Neighbors or borders
neighbors.description: Adjacent places, shared borders, nearby astronomical bodies, or neighboring realms.

routeConnections.label: Connected routes
routeConnections.description: Roads, passes, waterways, sea lanes, or space routes that connect this place to others.

tradePartners.label: Trade partners
tradePartners.description: Places or factions with active exchange.

controllingPowers.label: Controlled by
controllingPowers.description: Current powers able to govern, police, tax, occupy, or restrict the place.

claimants.label: Claimed by
claimants.description: Competing claims, inherited rights, occupation claims, or disputed sovereignty.

inhabitants.label: Inhabitants
inhabitants.description: Notable residents, species, cultures, or population groups modeled as entries.

founders.label: Founded by
founders.description: Founders, source colony, founding event, or patron power.

notableEvents.label: Notable events
notableEvents.description: Timeline events that happened here or materially changed the place.

relatedLore.label: Related lore
relatedLore.description: Magic, laws, customs, myths, ecology, artifacts, technologies, or hazards tied to this place.

scale.label: Scale
scale.description: Useful size descriptor such as global, regional, local, orbital, or extradimensional.

area.label: Area
area.description: Approximate area or footprint when useful.

population.label: Population
population.description: Approximate population, occupancy, seasonal population, or unknown status.

demographics.label: Demographics
demographics.description: Cultures, species, classes, languages, migration, and social divisions.

government.label: Government
government.description: Administrative structure, law, succession, offices, and authority model.

economy.label: Economy
economy.description: Production, trade, resources, labor systems, taxes, or scarcity.

defenses.label: Defenses
defenses.description: Walls, fleets, garrisons, fortifications, terrain advantages, wards, or patrols.

infrastructure.label: Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Roads, bridges, docks, water, sanitation, housing, communication, gates, or orbital systems.

districts.label: Districts or local areas
districts.description: Named districts, wards, enclaves, complexes, or local subareas.

climate.label: Climate
climate.description: Climate, weather pattern, atmosphere, or environmental feel.

terrain.label: Terrain
terrain.description: Physical shape, geology, ecology, visibility, footing, and travel conditions.

resources.label: Resources
resources.description: Natural resources, strategic assets, magical sources, food, water, minerals, or salvage.

hazards.label: Hazards
hazards.description: Natural, political, magical, technological, or social dangers.

access.label: Access
access.description: How entry, transit, landing, docking, crossing, or passage works.

travelTime.label: Travel time
travelTime.description: Typical time to cross, reach, orbit, sail, or traverse.

strategicValue.label: Strategic value
strategicValue.description: Military, political, economic, magical, navigational, or story value.

history.label: History
history.description: Origin, major eras, collapses, discoveries, renovations, disasters, and changes in control.

currentTension.label: Current tension
currentTension.description: Active conflict, scarcity, mystery, dispute, pressure, or instability.

secrets.label: Secrets
secrets.description: Hidden truth, contradiction, buried threat, false history, or story reveal.

sensoryDetails.label: Sensory details
sensoryDetails.description: Sights, sounds, smells, textures, weather, light, and first-impression details.

landmarks.label: Landmarks
landmarks.description: Important named landmarks located in this place.

waters.label: Waters
waters.description: Named water bodies related to this place.

source.label: Source or origin
source.description: Origin place, parent phenomenon, source event, spring, crater, gate, or myth.

mouth.label: Mouth or terminus
mouth.description: Where a flow, path, or route ends.

tributaries.label: Tributaries or feeders
tributaries.description: Inputs feeding a river, lake, sea, bay, glacier, magical current, or system.

floraFauna.label: Flora and fauna
floraFauna.description: Plants, animals, monsters, crops, fungal life, or engineered organisms.

magicOrTechnology.label: Magic or technology
magicOrTechnology.description: Local supernatural rules, machinery, infrastructure, anomalies, or limits.

condition.label: Condition
condition.description: Ruined, inhabited, contested, sealed, operational, abandoned, sacred, unstable, or other state.

builders.label: Built by
builders.description: Builders, engineers, original culture, patron, or construction event.

function.label: Function
function.description: Purpose in daily life, war, faith, transit, production, communication, research, or ritual.

tree.label: World
tree.summary: The broadest playable or narratively relevant setting container.
tree.notes: Use for a campaign world, known world, hollow world, or setting-scale map even when it is not a planet.

tree.label: Astronomical Space
tree.summary: Stellar and interstellar containers.
tree.notes: Grouping node used to keep the tree readable; it is not currently a selectable place category.

tree.label: Galaxy
tree.summary: A massive stellar collection and top-level space geography container.
tree.notes: Track political sectors as Region entries if they matter more than astronomical detail.

tree.label: Nebula
tree.summary: A cloud, stellar nursery, hazard zone, veil, or magical space phenomenon.
tree.notes: Useful for navigation hazards, concealment, rare resources, or divine/cosmic phenomena.

tree.label: Solar system
tree.summary: A star system containing stars, planets, moons, belts, stations, and routes.
tree.notes: Can also model a magical system orbiting a divine object rather than a scientific star.

tree.label: Star
tree.summary: A stellar body, sun, dead star, divine flame, or anchor of a system.
tree.notes: Fields should capture spectral, divine, magical, or navigational significance as appropriate.

tree.label: Planet
tree.summary: A planetary body that can contain continents, oceans, countries, moons, and settlements.
tree.notes: Use World when the exact planetary model is not relevant; use Planet when astronomical relationships matter.

tree.label: Moon
tree.summary: A natural or artificial satellite, inhabited moon, divine mirror, or tide driver.
tree.notes: There is no crater category yet; model major craters as Region, Canyon, Valley, or Ruin entries.

tree.label: Asteroid belt
tree.summary: A belt, debris field, mining zone, ring, or hazardous orbital region.
tree.notes: Can also stand in for planetary rings if ring detail matters.

tree.label: Space station
tree.summary: An orbital, deep-space, or extraplanar constructed habitat or facility.
tree.notes: Treat docking rings or internal wards as Port or Region entries when needed.

tree.label: Otherworldly Geography
tree.summary: Nonstandard cosmological containers used for fantasy realms and planes.
tree.notes: Grouping node used to separate cosmology from political geography.

tree.label: Dimension
tree.summary: A broad alternate reality, cosmological layer, dream-space, or metaphysical container.
tree.notes: Use access fields for gates, rituals, technologies, or conditions required to enter.

tree.label: Plane
tree.summary: A metaphysical layer, elemental plane, afterlife region, or divine jurisdiction.
tree.notes: Use for rule-bearing cosmological spaces whose physics or magic differs from the material world.

tree.label: Realm
tree.summary: A sovereign, magical, mythic, or culturally bounded domain that may not fit ordinary geography.
tree.notes: Good for fae courts, divine lands, cursed domains, or politically fuzzy fantasy territories.

tree.label: Terrestrial Macro Geography
tree.summary: Large land, water, and political containers on a world or planet.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: Continent
tree.summary: A major landmass containing countries, regions, biomes, mountain systems, waters, and settlements.
tree.notes: Political links are optional unless the continent is treated as a governed unit.

tree.label: Political and Administrative Places
tree.summary: Governed territories and named administrative divisions.
tree.notes: Grouping node; use to reason about fields shared by country, province, region, and kingdom.

tree.label: Country
tree.summary: A sovereign or recognized polity with territory, government, people, borders, and settlements.
tree.notes: Country editors should directly expose linked cities, provinces, borders, ruling factions, claimants, founding events, and major lore.

tree.label: Kingdom
tree.summary: A monarchy, royal domain, successor state, or crown-claimed territory.
tree.notes: Prefer Kingdom over Country when succession, dynasty, court politics, or crown legitimacy is central.

tree.label: Province
tree.summary: An administrative subdivision governed under a larger polity.
tree.notes: Use for satrapies, duchies, states, provinces, districts, counties, or administrative marches.

tree.label: Region
tree.summary: A flexible named area that can be political, cultural, ecological, magical, or practical.
tree.notes: This is the safest category for fuzzy or early-draft geography before a more precise category is known.

tree.label: Settlements
tree.summary: Inhabited places organized by scale and function.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: Capital
tree.summary: A seat of government, ritual authority, faction control, or symbolic central place.
tree.notes: A Capital can also be categorized as City in the prose; keep Capital when the capital role is the important fact.

tree.label: City
tree.summary: A large settlement with districts, institutions, trade, politics, and dense population.
tree.notes: Cities benefit from direct links to ruling factions, resident characters, districts, trade routes, nearby water, and founding events.

tree.label: Town
tree.summary: A mid-sized settlement with local governance, trade, and named institutions.
tree.notes: Towns often need sharper economic fields than cities because one industry or route usually defines them.

tree.label: Village
tree.summary: A small settlement defined by local families, work, nearby terrain, and social memory.
tree.notes: Villages should expose local customs, secrets, nearby hazards, notable families, and dependence on larger places.

tree.label: Hamlet
tree.summary: A tiny settlement, cluster of homes, outpost, camp, or named habitation point.
tree.notes: Hamlets may be seasonal, abandoned, hidden, or known only because a key character lives there.

tree.label: Maritime Settlements
tree.summary: Settlement categories defined by water access and docking function.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: Harbor
tree.summary: A sheltered water access point or harbor district used for anchorage, trade, repair, or defense.
tree.notes: Use Harbor when the physical shelter matters; use Port when the institution, customs, or dock infrastructure matters.

tree.label: Port
tree.summary: A managed docking, customs, trade, ferry, naval, river, or space-port facility.
tree.notes: Ports should link to the authorities, customs rules, route endpoints, cargo sources, and hazards that shape traffic.

tree.label: Built and Cultural Sites
tree.summary: Constructed or culturally defined places that often sit inside settlements or terrain.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: Fortress
tree.summary: A defensive military site, citadel, keep, border fort, orbital bastion, or fortified complex.
tree.notes: Fortresses are often relationship hubs for armies, commanders, sieges, roads, borders, and disputed claims.

tree.label: Castle
tree.summary: A noble residence, defensive seat, palace-fort, royal keep, or symbolic power center.
tree.notes: Castles should link to noble houses, rulers, heirs, staff, sieges, founding events, and nearby settlements.

tree.label: Temple
tree.summary: A sacred place, shrine, monastery, ritual complex, oracle, or institutional religious site.
tree.notes: Temple records should surface deity, rite, taboo, relic, faction, pilgrimage route, and access restrictions through lore and faction links.

tree.label: Ruin
tree.summary: An abandoned, destroyed, buried, haunted, or partially reclaimed former place.
tree.notes: Ruin fields should capture original function, fall event, current occupants, salvage, hazards, and false histories.

tree.label: Mine
tree.summary: A resource extraction site, quarry, shaft, asteroid operation, undercity dig, or magical harvesting site.
tree.notes: Mines need ownership, labor, output, depletion, hazards, transport, and ecological or magical consequences.

tree.label: Routes and Corridors
tree.summary: Places that primarily organize movement between other places.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: Road
tree.summary: A road, trail, causeway, caravan route, tunnel road, rail line, pilgrimage route, or known path.
tree.notes: There is no Bridge category yet; model important bridges as Road, Pass, or Landmark-style Region entries.

tree.label: Pass
tree.summary: A mountain pass, strait-like land gap, canyon crossing, gate corridor, or chokepoint.
tree.notes: Passes should expose seasonality, tolls, military control, closures, ambush sites, and alternate routes.

tree.label: Land Biomes and Terrain
tree.summary: Large terrestrial ecological and geological places.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: Forest
tree.summary: A wooded region with ecology, travel constraints, inhabitants, paths, and resources.
tree.notes: Forests need paths, canopy/light, ownership, taboos, resources, monsters, and border settlements.

tree.label: Jungle
tree.summary: A dense tropical or alien forest with intense growth, humidity, ruins, and travel friction.
tree.notes: Use Jungle instead of Forest when climate, density, disease, rivers, canopy, and hidden ruins are defining traits.

tree.label: Desert
tree.summary: An arid, icy, salt, ash, magical, or resource-scarce region shaped by exposure and routes.
tree.notes: There is no Oasis category yet; model important oases as Lake, Wetland, Village, or Region entries.

tree.label: Swamp
tree.summary: A wooded wetland, flooded lowland, boggy wilderness, or liminal hazard zone.
tree.notes: Swamps should capture water level, navigability, disease, concealment, guides, spirits, and seasonal change.

tree.label: Wetland
tree.summary: A marsh, fen, bog, delta wetland, reedland, or ecologically important water-land boundary.
tree.notes: Wetland is more ecological; Swamp is more atmospheric and wooded. Both can coexist if the distinction matters.

tree.label: Plains
tree.summary: A broad open low-relief region useful for agriculture, migration, cavalry, storms, or visibility.
tree.notes: Track pasture, grain, nomadic routes, battlefield visibility, weather exposure, and border vulnerability.

tree.label: Steppe
tree.summary: A dry grassland or semi-arid open region shaped by herding, migration, wind, and distance.
tree.notes: Often driven by cultures and routes more than fixed settlement density; link nomadic factions explicitly.

tree.label: Tundra
tree.summary: A cold treeless or sparsely vegetated region with permafrost, seasonal travel, and scarce resources.
tree.notes: Tundra records should capture seasonality, food, migration, exposure, and what makes travel possible.

tree.label: Mountain Systems
tree.summary: Highland and geological categories.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: Mountain range
tree.summary: A chain or system of mountains that shapes borders, climate, travel, watersheds, and cultures.
tree.notes: Mountain ranges are strong border and watershed entities; expose both adjacent polities and route passes.

tree.label: Mountain
tree.summary: A named peak, sacred mountain, isolated highland, or dominant landmark.
tree.notes: Mountains often need elevation, route difficulty, sacred status, resource value, and visibility from nearby places.

tree.label: Valley
tree.summary: A lowland corridor or basin between highlands, often with rivers, settlements, roads, and agriculture.
tree.notes: Valleys frequently become natural corridors, agricultural zones, or contested borderlands.

tree.label: Canyon
tree.summary: A deep cut, gorge, chasm, rift, or exposed geological corridor.
tree.notes: Canyons need vertical access, crossings, sight lines, floods, echoes, hidden routes, and what exposed the rock or ruin.

tree.label: Plateau
tree.summary: An elevated flatland or tableland with distinct climate, edges, access routes, and settlements.
tree.notes: Track edge routes and why the plateau is separated culturally, climatically, or militarily from lowlands.

tree.label: Volcano
tree.summary: An active, dormant, dead, magical, cryogenic, or divine volcanic feature.
tree.notes: Volcanoes should capture eruption history, warning signs, divine taboos, geothermal resources, and affected settlements.

tree.label: Glacier
tree.summary: A moving ice mass, ice sheet, frozen river, magical icefield, or climate marker.
tree.notes: Glaciers tie climate, time depth, water sources, hidden ruins, and dangerous travel into one record.

tree.label: Subterranean Places
tree.summary: Underground places and enclosed natural systems.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: Cave
tree.summary: A cave, cavern system, lair, tunnel network, shrine grotto, sinkhole, or underworld entrance.
tree.notes: Caves should capture entrances, depth, maps, air/water, inhabitants, acoustics, treasures, and why people go inside.

tree.label: Water and Coasts
tree.summary: Hydrological, coastal, and maritime geography.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: River
tree.summary: A river, stream, canal-like natural flow, magical current, lava river, or trade waterway.
tree.notes: Rivers should expose upstream/downstream links, crossings, settlements, navigability, floods, borders, and sacred or legal status.

tree.label: Lake
tree.summary: A freshwater, salt, crater, underground, glacial, magical, or artificial lake.
tree.notes: Lakes often need depth, water source, outflow, shoreline settlements, fishing rights, monsters, and seasonal freezing.

tree.label: Ocean
tree.summary: A world-scale body of water or analogous sea of sand, stars, mist, or magic.
tree.notes: Oceans are best modeled as route networks plus hazards, currents, cultures, weather, monsters, and trade lanes.

tree.label: Sea
tree.summary: A large named water body, inland sea, marginal sea, magical sea, or navigable subregion of an ocean.
tree.notes: Seas often define cultures, fleets, piracy, storms, fisheries, religious boundaries, and rival coastlines.

tree.label: Coast
tree.summary: A shoreline region where land and sea systems meet.
tree.notes: Coasts should link land polities to water bodies and expose trade, storms, raids, reefs, cliffs, beaches, and legal borders.

tree.label: Bay
tree.summary: A recessed coastal water body, sheltered anchorage, naval staging area, or culturally named inlet.
tree.notes: Bays are natural containers for ports, fleets, hidden coves, sea monsters, and contested naval access.

tree.label: Gulf
tree.summary: A large sea indentation or strategic maritime basin bordered by multiple coasts.
tree.notes: Gulfs usually matter because several powers share access, trade, resources, storms, or naval conflict.

tree.label: Island and Projecting Land
tree.summary: Landforms defined by surrounding water and coastal access.
tree.notes: Grouping node; not selectable in the current category list.

tree.label: Island
tree.summary: A landmass surrounded by water, lava, mist, void, ice, or magical boundary.
tree.notes: Islands should capture isolation, supplies, landing sites, sovereignty, storms, endemic life, and external dependency.

tree.label: Peninsula
tree.summary: A land projection surrounded mostly by water and connected to a larger landmass.
tree.notes: Peninsulas often need chokepoint, naval, border, and land-bridge fields.

tree.label: Archipelago
tree.summary: A group of islands treated as one geographic, political, trade, or cultural system.
tree.notes: Archipelagos should expose island-to-island routes, political fragmentation, currents, storms, and scarce resources.

implementationRecommendations.summary: Use category-specific field profiles to choose which fields display on the place editor.
implementationRecommendations.details: The current prototype can continue storing values in entry.fields while the UI chooses visible fields from fieldProfiles and node-specific importantLinks.

implementationRecommendations.summary: Represent linked fields as WorldRelationship records, not duplicated text.
implementationRecommendations.details: For example, a Country's settlements field should be derived from contains or located in relationships with City, Town, Village, Hamlet, Harbor, and Port place entries.
If a City is located in a Country, the Country editor should show that City under settlements or child places without requiring duplicate manual entry.

implementationRecommendations.summary: Treat target categories as suggestions during drafting.
implementationRecommendations.details: Fantasy settings often bend categories. The editor should warn or suggest rather than block unusual links.

implementationRecommendations.summary: Allow Region as an early placeholder that can later be refined.
implementationRecommendations.details: Many world records begin as vague regions. The model should preserve relationships when a Region becomes a Province, Forest, Realm, or other more specific category.

notes: Core entry fields are documented in the field catalog but are not rendered as place detail fields.
Shared detail fields render before a place category is selected.
